turns.thought: [OBSERVE] Iteration 913: no improvement for 200 iterations. Diversity (normalized) is 1.35e-11, position std ~1.3e-10, swarm radius ~9.0e-10. All particles have effectively identical positions (fitness mean/worst nearly equal to gbest 19.899, fitness std 1.53e-14). Velocity is zero for all particles (mean 5.4e-10, zero ratio 1.0). The swarm is frozen at a local minimum of the Rastrigin function. | [DIAGNOSE] The swarm has completely collapsed to a single point, a local minimum with value 19.899. This is classic premature convergence: the exploration pressure vanished long ago, and the particles' velocities are zero. The algorithm is unable to escape this basin without external perturbation. The root cause is insufficient diversity maintenance over the optimization run, leading to total stagnation. | [STRATEGIZE] The goal is to restore diversity and enable escape from the current basin. Randomly reinitializing a large fraction of particles (0.5) directly addresses the collapsed diversity by injecting fresh, uniformly distributed positions across the entire search space, while keeping the best solution as an anchor. Setting inertia to 1.1 will further encourage higher initial velocities to promote exploration. This is preferred over local perturbations (Gaussian mutation, Levy flights) because those would still remain near the stale gbest; the swarm needs a hard reset of a significant portion of the population.
